headline: Justice Department lawyer says concert ticket industry is broken because of Ticketmaster
summary: A U.S. Justice Department lawyer told jurors at a civil antitrust trial Tuesday that the concert industry is broken because Ticketmaster and its parent Live Nation Entertainment have monopolized the market, driving up prices for consumers, but the companies say the government's wrong.  David Dahlquist, an attorney with the Justice Department's antitrust division, said in his opening statement that the United States and 39 states are counting on the Manhattan federal jury to end the monopoly and reward artists and consumers with a competitive marketplace that will leave them with more money.  “Today, the concert ticket industry is broken.”

headline: UK launches competition probe into Hilton, IHG and Marriott
summary: The UK’s competition watchdog has opened a probe into Hilton, IHG and Marriott over concerns that shared hotel performance data may have reduced market competition.

raw.headline: UK launches competition probe into Hilton, IHG and Marriott
